message.content:
Package: ail.std.collections.

Type: Option<T>.
Option has variants:
- Some(value: T)
- None

Type: Result<T,E>.
Result has variants:
- Success(value: T)
- Failure(error: E)

Type: List<T>.
List has variants:
- Empty
- Items(values: T)

Type: Map<K,V>.
Map has variants:
- Empty
- Entries(key: K, value: V)

Type: Set<T>.
Set has variants:
- Empty
- Members(value: T)

Function: Option.map.
The function needs:
- option: Option<T>
- mapper: Text
The function produces:
- result: Option<U>
When Option.map runs:
- the system requires the option to exist
- the system checks if the option is Some(value)
- if the option is Some(value):
  - the system calls mapper with value
  - the system returns Some(mapped value)
  - the system records a trace event named OptionMapEvaluated
- if the option is None:
  - the system returns None
